reviews.review_text:
Used to play this game a hell of a lot on xbox before it came to steam, I can definitely feel the change in the times.
For TLDRs sake, if you're looking for a game about managing your own ship with total freedom in what you want to do that session with said ship, you've found it hands down. If you're looking to meet other pirates and plunder things together... run.

Starting with the pros, the game looks stylistically beautiful. Listening to the waves on the bow of your ship really gets you attached to it all. The freedom each voyage is quite nice, hunting megaladons one session then grabbing the wrong type of animal to deliver to a merchant that thought you had reading comprehension. Its fantastic

The cons. Biggest one hands down is the social interaction with other pirates. Earlier I mentioned change in the times. In its early and mid days, you'd meet other sailors, form an alliance, maybe play some instruments before dispersing onto the waves like drunk bacteria. 

Now? Every. Single. Ship. I have tried to grab that megaphone and say "hello!" instantly thinks im a Category 5 threat that needs instant destruction. So I thought "alright, it's probably going to be awful but lets try open-crew." Silent crew members staring at you, doing nothing for a while and quiting like they were forced to be there on a parol test.

Its a great pirate game, full of pirate stuff to pirate. But the only pirates left are the ones that no one wants to be around.
The devs supported a pdf file. The community managers knew about it and covered it up.

I fu-cking love Sea of Thieves. But this adoration comes with a huge caveat: Only when I play it with my friends. 

Playing this game with a full galleon of my favourite people in a Discord call together is some of the most fun I've EVER had in a videogame and I think those moments are genuinely unmatched. So much so that I have 100 hours in this game and maybe 5 of them were just me on my own. So much so that I made TWO hours of YouTube videos of just me and my friends messing around in the game and accomplishing nothing specific. We played so much of this game we have developed specific terminology to describe things in game and our own methodology to completing tasks and farming resources. 

I genuinely adore this game so much and yet at 112GBs of size I just cannot justify reinstalling this game when I can't guarantee that I'll be able to get everyone on board (haha get it?) in a world where Sea of Thieves literally has a paid battlepass and seasonal challenges in a game that costs 40 f-ucking Great British Pounds. 

I love so much about this game, if you are in a group of good friends just looking to mess around and maybe sink a few noobs but at the same time, the rampant and blatant cheaters, absolutely no-life sweats that take you down in 4 seconds flat, and again, incredibly predatory monetisation is very off-putting. 

I give this game so many passes but new content recently has been so sparse and flavourless that it really is hard to endorse as a returning player. 

I have marked this game as recommended but please Rare, Microsoft, whoever is at the helm (heheh GET IT GET IT?) please sort out the constant teleporting pirate ninjas and subpar updates.

Community manager protected a predator and even uplifted them to a position where they could more easily access and exploit minors. Situation was being so badly mishandled that official partners are stepping down. Abhorrent situation and a dismal response to it by Rare.

I have played this game for over 2300 hours as of posting this review, I used to see the beauty in the details put into the world, loved interacting with other players, and adored everything from the story mode missions to the on-demand PvP. All that changed after senior community managers were running cover for a predator (Bruno) and he was exposed using his positions of social prominence and official promotion by the community team at Rare on the website, forums, and YouTube to groom and endanger children. Do not buy this game, do not support Rare with any future projects, and do not support any content that comes out in this game with microtransactions (which they have increasingly shoved down players' throats anyways).

Spent a lot of hours enjoying this game but it's like the dev team is just bent on making the game worse. Content updates are focused on squeezing as much money out of players as possible, often at the cost of punishing long-time players. Modifying items that people worked a very long time for to be inherently worse, removing currency from players who earned it fairly.

Also worth noting that multiple times the devs and community managers have elevated pedophiles that prey on the game's community into positions of trust, ignored calls to action when wrongdoing was found and labored in the interest of protecting these bad actors and limiting their own legal liability rather than providing transparency and taking action against those responsible. 

I'm absolutely disgusted. We're past "do better" and we have arrived at "pack it in"

Fire your community management. Replace your development team with people interested in the longevity of the game and ensuring it is fun to play. The negligence and lack of accountability here are absolutely breathtaking. Of course we know this all falls on deaf ears.

You had something incredible here, lightning in a bottle. You've thrown it all away.

A good pirate experience, beautiful sailing mechanics and water, low hardware requirements, fun dungeons and treasuring grabbing experiences. Main downside is that it's a "friends" game, you can technically play it solo, but it wont be that fun compared to a group play session. Overall a pretty good game to buy when it's half off or more ($20 or less), really wish it was smaller though, 130gb for this is crazy.

At it's core, the game really has wonderful aspects to it, the art direction and world building being to me it's biggest strong suits along with sailing and the sense of grandure and seemingly endless exploration across the seas.
Sounds awesome, doesn't it? Well it is, until the community enters the chat.

    I want to preface, I have had some great interactions with encountered crews and awesome, chaotic, long-winded but well-spirited fights across the many hours that I've spent playing this game and this is truly another aspect where the game shines, but they are far outweighed by the slew of basement dwelling, unwashed, unloved and unbearably toxic PVP sweats populating the seas solely to vent their frustration, people with no other ambition than ruining whatever fun you are trying to get out of the game, people more interested in chasing you for literal HOURS around the map not even for your loot or XP but only to spawn camp and trash talk you as you are helplessly getting looped between your ship and the ferry of the damned, until you scuttle to a new server or just flat-out ALT + F4, to the many people who will undoubtably comment something along the lines of "Bro just git gud, this isn't Sea of Friends, this is a pirate game kill or be killed, Rolling on deck laughing." you are exactly the type of people this section of the review is aimed at, go play Counter Strike or be a good little cuck and go play some Call of Duty if your only motivation is to ruin what little fun people can find in the shallow, repetitive and soul crushing grind which can get ripped away from you in a matter of seconds that this game offers.

    If you're a new player hoping for some casual fun with your friends and want to find what little enjoyment there is left in the game that doesnt involve PVP just save your money, "Then just go on safer seas then you filthy casual", sure... If it wasnt completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ useless because of the company level cap, the lack of emisarry flag to earn extra gold and the VERY FEW good interactions you MIGHT actually have once in a while when playing on high seas, oh also, NOT BEING ABLE TO USE THE CAPTAINED SHIP I BUSTED MY ASS FOR.

    The worst part of all of this is that this is just the tip of the iceberg, the report system is completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ useless, getting called every slur under the sun is apparently "not enough information" when reporting someone, NOTHING is done to balance out lobbies or PVP matches to prevent new players from getting plowed by veterans, said veterans of the game are getting more and more alienated by each new update cycle released by a completely community-disconected dev team who, might I add, actively protect PEDOPHILES in their official community management team, and make every single ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ update they release meant to add new paid cosmetics (which is admittedly less than before to be fair), more FOMO content, more half assed ♥♥♥♥ the community never even asked for, back pedaling teased projects that the community actually has interest for, all the while breaking their game adding glitches that are laughable at best or absolutely catastrophic like, oh I don't know, COMPLETELY REMOVE CONTROL EXCEPT YOUR CAMERA'S VERTICAL AXIS WHEN IN THE WATER or even better FORBIDDING YOU FROM ACCESSING ANYTHING ON YOUR SHIP losing you fights and literal HOURS of progress and effort, fighting for you life to get done dirty by a team that clearly doesnt give a ♥♥♥♥ about their game anymore and let's it survive on life support.

    In the end, Sea of Thieves is a strange beast, I really do love it, but the combination of this identity crisis ♥♥♥♥ the team is having, the state of PVP as a whole, and the clearly apparent lack of ♥♥♥♥ the team gives for the time you invest in their game is just completely baffling for a product with such high highs, but when there are lows, HOLY ♥♥♥♥ are they low.

PS: this has been going on for literal YEARS, from before I even started playing, sure some issues might have gotten fixed like the rampant cheating issue from a few years back (from what i can tell at least, I couldn't say for sure if I have encountered cheaters), but this is just a fraction of problems that are barely if EVER even addressed by the devs, get your ♥♥♥♥ together Rare.
The Sea of Thieves community managers and mods defends pedophiles, DO NOT PLAY, NOT RECOMMENDED FOR CHILDREN.

Garbo, forget the pedo claims, you cant even call someone "stupid" ingame without getting a temp ban from the game. I knew in season 2 that this ♥♥♥♥ was not gonna work. bad company that disliked bad words gets caught for covering for pedos. jeez, who woulda guessed?

I loved this game, but the Sea of Thieves has been suffering from constant disappointing delivery of content, failed promises, and far too many "we've missed the mark, we'll do better". It feels like the game is on it's deathbed and is being beat in order to speed up the process. Rare continues to make the same mistakes over and over, with zero improvements. The game, for years now, has been running on hopes and dreams that have been slowly dying out. the content and how it is presented has become lackluster. one month worth of updates released over three. and the performance has become unbearable. You couldn't go a single session without some form of lag, rubber banding, or the god forsaken hit-reg issues popping up. And i would be able to forgive this, if Rare would actually improve on these problems instead of claiming to fix them, and doing nothing that helps. Finally, Rare themselves are a problem. They hired and promoted a pedophile into a community management position, having already known what kind of monster he was. And Rare's response has been the most corporate, cookie-cutter response ive ever seen, and now, they are trying to push it under the rug to avoid accountability for hiring such a monstrous person. Rare has made me regret supporting them because of there recent actions, and I can not, in good conscience, recommend this game to anyone. Don't support pedophiles, and don't support those who enable them.

Rare employee's are covering up pedophilic actions and bad actors in their community.

Right now, I'd hold off on playing this game until they get a new community manager and they kick Bruno out, if you know you know. :skull:

The fact that it's gotten to a point where even partners are threatening to drop the partnership if accountability isn't taken should be enough of a point. Make your voices clear, if this doesn't pull a sudden 180 and accountability isn't taken. Scuttle the ship.

Sea of Thieves delivers one of the most unique pirate experiences available today. From sailing across vast oceans to hunting treasure, battling skeletons, and engaging in intense naval combat, the game creates moments that few other multiplayer titles can match. The art style is beautiful, the world feels alive, and the ocean physics remain some of the best in gaming.

Playing with friends is where Sea of Thieves truly shines. Coordinating a crew, taking on world events, and fighting rival pirates can lead to unforgettable adventures and plenty of laughs. The freedom to create your own stories is one of the game's greatest strengths.

Unfortunately, the experience is held back by several issues. The gameplay loop can become repetitive over time, with many activities feeling similar after dozens of hours. Solo players may struggle against larger crews, and progression is mostly limited to cosmetic rewards. The game also suffers from occasional bugs and technical issues, ranging from minor annoyances to glitches that can disrupt gameplay and take away from the overall experience.

While the PvP system adds excitement and tension, losing hard-earned loot to another crew can sometimes feel more frustrating than rewarding. Despite years of updates and new content, Sea of Thieves still relies heavily on players creating their own fun rather than providing a strong sense of progression.

Overall, Sea of Thieves is a fun and visually impressive pirate sandbox that offers some incredible multiplayer moments. However, repetitive gameplay, limited progression, and occasional bugs prevent it from fully living up to its potential.

Final Rating: 6.5/10

I've played this game a lot on and off over the years. I've loved this game. I've had many fun adventures with my friends on the high seas hunting for treasure, looting islands, going on quests, fighting ghosts, skeletons, megalodons, krakens and of course other players. I've done some tall tales, and earned millions in gold, but I can tell you that never have I gotten excited for doubloons. They have not been a currency in the game with much worth to spending them on. So of course, the devs have finally decided to put stuff into the shop worth spending doubloons on and made a brilliant decision; reward everyone's hard work on earning their hard earned doubloons over the years by [b]resetting everyone's doubloons[/b] and [b]erasing all the players hard work[/b] on getting them. You read that correctly. Your time playing their game means [b]nothing[/b] to the dev team. Or at least, that's effectively what resetting your doubloons says to me.

Now all of that was enough for me to finally say I think I've had enough with this game. It's time to move on, but the devs decided that wasn't stupid enough. While they decided to reset everyone's doubloons and add a hard cap to the amount of doubloons you could earn, they also changed how you could earn them by adding an event to turn in specific chests for doubloons and this event also happens to be where these new shops to spend doubloons are open. The problem with this other than what was stated previously, is that you only had [b]3 days[/b] to collect and spend doubloons during the event. Not only that, but the devs didn't reset everyone before the event like they were planning. So some people were reset and some were not and because of the cap, those that did not reset could not earn more doubloons until they spent what they had anyway. How did the devs respond? They took away everyone's purchases during the event which is now over and you can't collect doubloons or buy anything with them until the next one which they do once a month. So they punished everyone for their own mistake.

To top everything off I've now been seeing that they've allegedly been protecting pedophiles within their discord, so I don't think I can recommend this game anymore. Not without significant changes to how this dev team operates and treats their player base.

Game has been declining in quality over the past few years and it's clear Rare has no idea what they want to do with the game at all and are just adding things for the sake of it with no clear vision, or in their words, "we've missed the mark."

Rare is now locking down the Discord and haven't said anyrhing about it yet in the last few days since it came to light

Amazing pirate vibe, but highly frustrating for solo players.
​I really want to love this game because the sailing and world-building are top-notch. However, as a solo player, the experience is underwhelming compared to games like AC Black Flag where you have full control over your ship.
​In Sea of Thieves, if you want to sail a bigger ship, you are forced to play multiplayer with real people. Matchmaking with strangers isn't always reliable, and not everyone has a group of friends available to play. Why not allow solo players to fully control larger ships, even if it requires a heavy grind to unlock that privilege?
​It feels like the game punishes you just for playing alone. Great game overall, but needs better balance for solo captains.

But I see the community's complaints about the current state of the game and, even though it's generally about things that don't affect my experience that much, I can see why everyone's so upset. Game's been lagging behind A LOT when it comes to quality updates, the devs keep missing Mark (I hope he's back soon), there's a strong push for microtransactions all the time, and the gameplay options for PVE players are VERY limited (this is more of a me problem, but still).

But the worst part is the way recent Discord-related events are being handled. Won't get into detail, everyone knows what's happening.

I really wanted to like it but I can’t really say I did. I liked the idea, but at least while I was playing updates didn’t feel substantial and the game was more of a grind than feeling like a pirate adventure. It was fun to do stupid ♥♥♥♥ with my friends for a while but that was about all the fun it had after a while.

I used to play this game lots and decide to come back recently, Its not bad as a game and is fun every once in a while and with friends it can be a blast for a while, but the developers are so slow at adding anything interesting to do and when they do its mostly just reused content. The grind to get anything interesting is just doing the same content over and over for the different factions just with a different loot reward and reputation system to grind. Eventually the cost of any interesting cosmetics gets ridiculously absurd costing millions for one ship part. They said they fixed the pirate legend grind and made it more rewarding, but the only thing your rewarded with once you reach it after so much grinding is more grinding and more absurd cost ship pieces. The only fun thing to do eventually is to hunt and sink other players, but most of the players in this game just run away and its not fun to chase for hours just to sink them and they have nothing on the ship, couple this with horrible server lag and you just don't have a fun time constantly having to fight the lag more than the players. Finally the game constantly screws you and you get "Rared" constantly, while performing other tasks you'll get interrupted constantly by random events. I have been chasing players and constantly get krakened or a meg spawns. I one sank a player after 2 hours of chasing and as soon as I sank them a Meg spawned and I fought it, after that a skeleton sloop and galleon spawned on me before I could even grab the megs loot after I sank the sloop before I sank the galleon another galleon spawned. This led to a very defeated moral and just not fun having to constantly be sidetracked. All in all the game is fun but gets boring very quick, coupled with slow updates and the constant reusing of content instead of actual new stuff, and the laggy servers, I would recommend give it a try if you really like pirate games and have friends but If your unsure on if you would, I would personally recommend you get a different game.

Might as well be 100 gigabytes of wasted storage
SOT used to be one of my favorite games to just chill with friends on, but it frankly feels like the devs don't care about what a travesty the modern SOT experience is. It's just so... boring. 
Maybe this is just because I don't really (or at all) like the combat experience, but the game gets so repetitive so quickly that often me and my friends find ourselves filling up an emissary flag, selling all of our loot, and booting up a better game. None of the voyages/missions really offer any replayability beyond the first encounter and the tall tales are all massively undercooked. Sure, I absolutely adore the wild rose sets, but I don't think it was worth doing the same boring tall tale ten times in a row. 
Especially after the recent... controversy... I don't think that I can bring myself to play SOT again. There's just no point, and there hasn't been one in a long time.

I have around 1,900 hours in Sea of Thieves: 1,200 on Steam, and about 700 more from Xbox and the pre-Steam PC version.

I loved this game. That is why this sucks to write.

When Sea of Thieves works, there is nothing else like it. Sailing with friends, getting into stupid fights over treasure, meeting random crews, escaping with loot by pure luck. Those moments are the reason I kept coming back for so long.

But I cannot recommend it anymore.

The servers feel unreliable, cheating is a real problem, and the client has gotten frustrating. On PC, my performance is awful now: the game runs over 60 FPS, then drops to around 5 FPS every couple of seconds, while my CPU, RAM, and GPU are all sitting below 40%. Even on Xbox Series X, I have had bugs stop me from reloading cannons and selecting food to heal. Sometimes dying or force-quitting was the only temporary fix.

I also reported a Pirates Life Tall Tale bug around two years ago, and in my experience it still has not been fixed.

The FOMO makes it worse. You can pay for a pass, but if you do not play enough during that season, you can miss rewards you already paid for. The new Season Pass direction sounds even more frustrating, with paid progression boosts and paid level skips. That does not feel like respecting my time or my wallet. It feels like the game is trying to schedule my free time for me.

Sea of Thieves still has magic in it. I would not have played nearly 2,000 hours if it did not.

But right now, I spend too much time fighting bugs, stutters, cheaters, bad servers, and FOMO instead of just enjoying the game.

I hope it gets better, but I cannot recommend Sea of Thieves anymore.

A formerly quite good game that has, sadly, fallen victim to ever-waning updates, unresolved technical issues, and general... atrophy. 

This alone would not be enough for me to outright recommend against a game, plenty of older games with limited support can still hold much appeal, but in light of the recent debacle with a child predator being pushed through the review process by Rare employees and put in an elevated position in the community, despite numerous lingering allegations, I can only advise to stay well away from this game. 

While the person in question has been removed, after much backlash, the Community Managers involved in putting them in such a position, despite other Boatswains voting in majority against such a thing, still remain, and Rare continues to actively censor discussion of the issue on their platforms. Do not give this company, so willing to protect and harbour known creeps (and then try and sweep it under the rug), your money or your time.
This company loves pedophiles, and it’s a kids game, so that should tell you everything that you need to know!

This game is horrible don't buy it. 

I've played sea of thieves for quite a while on Xbox and it's got an even worse in recent years. 

It's hard to make any kind of progress in this game because there's roving bands of players that will just sink your ship and take what you got, even if you have absolutely nothing. 

To be fair the game is called see if thieves, so that's to be expected, but there's also a private mode or single player mode so to speak. But there might as well not be because they've added AI pirates to the game. So you can be playing in private mode, but at any time you'll get attacked by AI pirates and it usually happens when you have a little bit of treasure on your ship. I've even been attacked at Outpost while my ship was anchored, and somehow they were able to bypass the anchor and move my ship. 

This game is a complete f****** time waste. Go play windrose. Go play anything else that respects your time more that you're not going to lose hours of progress on because the game hates people that play solo. 

Now if you plan to play in the group, then you'll probably have a good time. But if you're not always in a group setting. You'll either get destroyed by players, or you'll get destroyed by the AI who pops up on a big ass ghost ship with a huge crew where you just have absolutely no chance and you can't even outrun it.

WARNING DON'T BUY THIS PIECE OF ♥♥♥♥ GAME

The playerbase is either people just learning the game or a bunch of idiots in a party being toxic. It's mostly the ladder :)

Save your time and don't buy this ♥♥♥♥ game. It's dying for a reason.

Honestly it's very sad to see this game going from one of my most favorite & played games on my Xbox with 100 days played to seeing it implode on its self. I remember when Rare actually listened to the community. Before Seasons, Battlepasses, and Microtransactions where all they cared about. I remember when they didn't reskin every new item to the in game stores. I remember when having a certain cosmetic actually meant something and it had actual value instead of re re-releasing it years later; making it less valuable. I remember when updates were full of exciting and new lore, stories, and features. Instead now all we get is lackluster updates that: "Missed the Mark."

Edit: Right after posting I forgot to mention that They keep changing Season Structure with limited time story adventures that left out chunks of the story if you missed them. No new Tall Tales. Acts making updates feel empty and unfinished too. 

This game has been on a downward spiral ever sense Covid. I left SoT when I got to the point when the only time I played the game is to avoid Fear Of Missing Out. And I would grind the game even when I didn't want to play it to finish the season pass or get a limited time cosmetic. At some point around 2 years ago I just stopped playing because I wasn't going to let the FOMO force me to play a game that lost its spark. After seeing the players get smaller & smaller and the updates get worse & worse. The doubloon reset getting rid of my hoard of doubloons and it absolutely failing in every metric. Plus the Bruno Files, Cheaters, And ignoring the Fanbase. 

I also see SoT content creators (Capt. Falcore) defending rare when it comes to hate and I have to say that they deserve it at this point, they don't listen to us at all and only listen to content creators. When they fail they say "We've missed the Mark" and we promise to listen to feedback. But they never do and it's been like this for years! Stop letting them get away with constantly lying to us. I have to say I have never seen a game ruin its reputation like Sea of Thieves and it makes me angry and sad that one of my favorite games turned out like this.

Edit: I forgot to mention that they keep changing the seasons and story structure with Tall Tales, Adventures, and now Acts. Which makes the story very confusing to understand and navigate. They also slowly abandoned the story of SoT which was one of the main reasons I loved this game.
the community managers protect pdf files, look up the Bruno situation on youtube and yall will see

rare is messing up, allowing bad actors in their ranks and killing the communities trust. They do not listen to feedback from players and release broken content into the game that makes a bunch of bugs

With over 500 hours logged on this game, I can't say I dislike it, I do genuinely enjoy this game and the fun I've had with friends while playing it. I just can't see myself picking up the game again because of the death spiral I've seen happening with every new season. The large amount of bugs were funny, I don't mind that, but the constant FOMO this game runs on as well as the feature-creep that comes with every season makes trying to complete commendations or any other task in this game feel worthless because for every achievement you complete, a new page of commendations will be added to greet you. That alongside the failure that was the currency reset makes me want to pick up the game less, knowing that all my hard work collecting doubloons over my 500 hours meant nothing. I do hope there will be some redeeming aspects added in future or even removal of unneeded features to make the game as fun as it was when I played it for the first time.

not even gonna write a funny review, this is just a good game. the water is stunning, the art style is iconic, the gameplay is nice, the navigation is interesting, and it's great to play with friends. not the most beginner friendly if you're playing singleplayer, but there's a lot of chill people in the community (ive met a decent few on discord) who are willing to help newbies.

I raid an Ashen Garrison, and get a bunch of loot. I find a Black Powder Barrel. I decide to take it with me to sell to smugglers. Omw to sell loot, skeleton sloop attacks me. I sink it and go over to loot it like anyone else would. Accidently runs over a black powder barrel I didn't see that somehow didn't explode on the skeleton sloop when fighting it. I die in the explosion and my sloop sinks. I respawn with a brand new ship and go over to where my old ship sank. I get lucky and see all the loot is still there. As I get close to loot it I run over another black powder barrel, that somehow didn't explode in the initial explosion. I die and my ship sinks again, along with my loot. In conclusion, I utterly despise the black powder barrel and whoever came up with the concept of it should be publicly shamed and should be forced to play one ranked game of LoL as punishment. 8/10 game will play again.
this game really makes you feel like a pirate. being a pirate is very fun. therefore, this game is fun.

I played the game for quite a few years now. Update after update the game got more bugs. They updated a 1vs1 ship battle to a last ship standing mode with 6 ships. That sounds great doesn't it? It was great, but then we encountered even more bugs. This time we got in an existing round, the ship spinned like a fidget spinner, then we lost, then the round actually started, then there were only 2 ships the game told us, after that we saw, that there were 3 ships, then we won, but the round didn't end. While we waited we saw, that there was no boarder around the map.
So in the end we had to leave and just wasted 1 hour.
If you play, pls bring at least 4 hours for one world event or mission and to deal with the bugs.

PS: I hope you didn't fall asleep reading my grammaticly correct review. c:
